response.justification: The candidate brings genuinely relevant geospatial experience (founding GISCollective, building GIS applications for Green Map) and proven low-latency systems work in DLang handling millions of requests per second, which demonstrates the systems-engineering mindset this role needs. However, modern C++ experience is thin and dated — a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis — and recent years have been focused on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than C++/Rust pipeline engineering. There is no direct routing or navigation guidance experience. Worth a conversation given the geospatial domain fit and systems background, but the core language requirement is a real gap.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial data platform experience as founder of GISCollective and GIS work for Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, caching, fibers) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing tooling authorship
concerns: Modern C++ experience is limited and roughly a decade old; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience
Recent roles (2020-present) are web/full-stack TypeScript, drifting away from systems programming
